questions.question: Explain the difference between getParameter() and getAttribute() methods in HttpServletRequest. Provide a scenario where each would be used.
keyPoints: getParameter() retrieves data sent by client (form data, query strings) - always returns String
getAttribute() retrieves data set by server-side code (objects) - can return any Object type
getParameter() returns null if parameter doesn't exist; getAttribute() returns null if attribute doesn't exist
Example: getParameter() for login form username; getAttribute() for data from database query passed to JSP

questions.question: What is the purpose of setCharacterEncoding() in servlet request handling? When must it be called, and what happens if you forget to call it?
keyPoints: Sets the character encoding for decoding request body parameters
Must be called BEFORE any getParameter() methods
If forgotten, default encoding (often ISO-8859-1) is used, causing garbled characters for non-ASCII input (like UTF-8)
Only affects POST request body parameters, not GET parameters (which use URL encoding)

questions.question: Differentiate between forward() and sendRedirect() methods in servlet handling. Include the HTTP behavior and URL changes in your answer.
keyPoints: forward() is server-side; RequestDispatcher forwards request to another resource
sendRedirect() sends HTTP 302/307 status to browser, browser makes new request
forward() - single request, URL unchanged (browser doesn't know)
sendRedirect() - two requests, URL changes to the redirect location
forward() is faster; sendRedirect() useful for redirecting to external URLs

questions.question: Write the code snippet to handle a POST request containing form data with UTF-8 encoding and return a JSON response.
keyPoints: Set character encoding first: request.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8")
Read parameters using getParameter()
Set response content type: response.setContentType("application/json")
Set character encoding for response: response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8")
Write JSON using response.getWriter() or getOutputStream()

questions.question: How do you retrieve all header names and values from an HTTP request in a servlet? Explain with code.
keyPoints: Use getHeaderNames() which returns Enumeration<String>
Iterate through header names using hasMoreElements() and nextElement()
For each header name, use getHeader() to get the value
Common headers: Content-Type, Authorization, Accept, Cookie

questions.question: Explain the concept of request attributes in servlets. How are they different from session attributes? Provide use cases for each.
keyPoints: Request attributes exist only during a single request-response cycle
Set using setAttribute(), retrieve using getAttribute()
Session attributes persist across multiple requests from the same client
Use request attributes for forwarding data between servlets/JSPs in same request
Use session attributes for user login state, shopping cart across pages

questions.question: Design a servlet that handles both GET and POST requests for a login form. The servlet should validate credentials and either redirect to a success page with user data in session, or redirect back to login with an error message. Include proper error handling and encoding.
keyPoints: Implement doGet() to show login form (setContentType, write HTML)
Implement doPost() to process login (setCharacterEncoding first)
Validate input parameters (check null/empty)
Authenticate user (hardcoded or mock DB check)
On success: set session attribute, redirect to success page (sendRedirect)
On failure: set error as request attribute, forward to login with error
Use proper character encoding throughout

questions.question: Explain the complete lifecycle of an HTTP request in a servlet container. Include details about when the service() method is called, how it dispatches to doGet/doPost, and the role of HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse objects.
keyPoints: Client sends HTTP request to server
Container creates HttpServletRequest and HttpServletResponse objects
Container identifies correct servlet and creates/uses existing instance
Container calls service() method, passing request and response
service() determines HTTP method and calls doXxx() (doGet, doPost, etc.)
Servlet processes request using request methods, generates response
Response is sent back to client
Request/Response objects are recycled or garbage collected
Request attributes are cleared after each request
